title: Update project charter and CLAUDE.md to reflect modular data architecture
description: The project charter currently commits to flat, explicit code structure as a constraint on abstraction. The decision to split data.js by domain needs to be reconciled with that commitment and the rationale documented: flat-within-a-file remains the standard; the file count increase is driven by LLM context efficiency, not architectural layering. CLAUDE.md should be updated with selective loading guidance — which data file to load for which task type. This is also a DESIGN.md-worthy methodology note (architecture shaped by toolchain constraints).
close_reason: Closed

title: Modularise data.js into domain files
description: data.js is growing toward unmanageable size and loads entirely into LLM context even when only a small section is needed. Split into domain files: data/professors.js (professor objects + their moves), data/playerMoves.js, data/students.js (student NPC battle characters + npcMoves pool), data/ambientNpcs.js, data/items.js, data/regions.js, data/dialogue.js. Update all import paths in battle.js, game.js, and any other consumers. No barrel/index.js re-export — update imports explicitly so the selective-loading benefit is real. Do this before the ee1 data additions land, since ee1 will add three more large arrays.
close_reason: Closed

title: Write BattleScene unit tests with Phaser mock
description:
BattleScene.js has no automated test coverage. This issue tracks writing a full test suite for the battle scene using a Phaser mock strategy.

Approach: create tests/mocks/phaser.mock.js that stubs Phaser.Scene and all game object methods (add.text, add.graphics, tweens, input.keyboard, time, cameras, scene, sound, textures, load). This makes BattleScene importable in the test harness without a running Phaser game.

Coverage targets:
- battleState initialisation (menuLevel default, playerMoves filtered to ACTIVE_MOVE_IDS, HP values)
- renderMoveMenu() / renderActionMenu() — correct text content set on mock objects
- _updateMoveDesc() — correct description set for each selected move index
- resolveTurn() damage calculations, move effects (skip, player_recoil, halve_next, counter, disrupt, deferred), win/loss branching
- Menu state transitions: Fight opens move submenu, ESC returns to action menu, post-turn returns to action menu
- flee() sets outcome and phase correctly

Deliverables: tests/mocks/phaser.mock.js, tests/battle.test.js, updated tests/index.html to include the new test file.
close_reason: 26 tests written using phaser-stub.js + Object.create strategy; covers _applyDeferredDamage, _applyPlayerMove, _applyProfessorTurn, _setUIMode

title: Implement js/scenes/BattleScene.js
description: Implement the turn-based battle scene per tdd/battle.md. Receives professorId via scene init data. Renders professor sprite, player sprite, HP bars (Graphics), move menu (Text), and battle log (Text). Handles move selection, damage resolution, move effects (disrupt, self_damage, deferred), flee, and win/loss detection. On battle end, wakes OverworldScene. Initial milestone: playable battle against prof_schwaartz with music.
close_reason: js/scenes/BattleScene.js implemented per TDD; AudioScene dependency is a runtime call only — code is complete; all move effects from data.js handled including skip, player_recoil, clear_debuff, halve_next, counter beyond the three in TDD

title: Implement js/scenes/AudioScene.js
description: Implement the persistent audio scene per tdd/audio.md. Loads all tracks from data.audioTracks in preload(). Adds them as Phaser sound instances in create() and marks the scene as active but invisible so it persists across other scene transitions. Exposes switchTo(trackId), play(trackId), and stop(). Victory and defeat tracks can be no-ops until the files are sourced.
close_reason: js/scenes/AudioScene.js implemented per TDD; all tracks loaded from audioTracks, persistent via setActive(true).setVisible(false), switchTo/play/stop exposed

title: Implement index.html and js/main.js
description: index.html is the game entry point — loads vendor/phaser.min.js and js/main.js as a module. main.js creates the Phaser.Game instance with the config from tdd/game.md: dimensions 480x320, scenes [OverworldScene, BattleScene, DialogueScene, AudioScene], arcade physics with no gravity. For the battle scene milestone, only BattleScene and AudioScene need to be registered; OverworldScene and DialogueScene can be stubbed.
close_reason: index.html and js/main.js implemented; Phaser 480x320 config with all 4 scenes registered; OverworldScene and DialogueScene stubbed for battle milestone

title: Implement js/engine.js
description: Implement the game state module per tdd/engine.md and briefs/engine.md. Pure state — no rendering, no input. Exports: init, getState, setPlayerHP, setPlayerPosition, setRegion, setScene, setPendingEncounter, defeatProfessor, isDefeated, allProfessorsDefeated, resetGame.
close_reason: js/engine.js implemented per TDD and brief; all 10 exports present, pure state, no rendering or input logic

title: Download Phaser 3 to vendor/phaser.min.js
description: The game requires Phaser 3 as a local file (no CDN) to satisfy the no-build-step, no-server constraint. Download phaser.min.js from the Phaser 3 release and place it at vendor/phaser.min.js. Create the vendor/ directory. Record the version in a vendor/README.md.
close_reason: phaser.min.js v3.90.0 downloaded to vendor/; vendor/README.md created with version record

title: Rewrite TDDs and charter for Phaser 3 migration
description: The project is switching from vanilla JS canvas rendering to Phaser 3. All six rendering/orchestration TDDs (game, map, battle, dialogue, audio, visuals) need rewriting to describe Phaser Scene architecture. The project charter external library section needs a documented vetting decision for Phaser. The deliverables note on DEL-003 needs updating. data.md and engine.md are unchanged.
close_reason: All TDDs and charter rewritten for Phaser 3 architecture

title: Implement the playable game
description: Implement all seven JS modules (game, engine, data, map, battle, dialogue, audio) per the TDD files, producing a playable browser game that opens via index.html with no build step. All six professors must be encounter-able, battles must resolve correctly, and the full core loop must be completable end-to-end.

title: Conceptual grounding
notes: Conduct required research to populate background.md and project_charter.md; both were scaffolded with placeholders at init. Populate incrementally as the project develops.
close_reason: game_design.md authored with all six professors, campus map, battle system, dialogue system, audio, and weather sections complete.

title: Implement item system (inventory, consumable use, effect resolution)
description: No item system exists yet. Needs: inventory array in game state tracking held items and quantities; consumable use (in battle and/or overworld — scope TBD); effect resolution for all effect types (HP restore, attack boost, exp boost, defence boost); and key item state flags (hasIdCard, hasSecretCode) that other systems can check. Persistent items (Star Gourd, Emotional Support Pickle) apply their effect immediately on acquisition and persist for the session. HP restore values for consumables are currently TBD in the GDD and will need a balancing pass.

title: Implement student NPC battle encounters
description: Wire student NPCs as battlable opponents in the overworld and battle scene. Requires: encounter tiles for each student NPC added to the relevant region in data.js, the battle scene extended to load studentNPC data (HP, moves, dialogue) alongside the existing professor path, dialogue sequences added for all 15 battle students, and post-battle reward logic (item grants for Marcellus → Star Gourd, Suzanna → Dialecters Badge, Lionel → Music League Badge, Composita → Chicken Run Badge). Special moves (Room Booking Rant, Access Denied, Question!, Meaning Crisis, Colloquium Circuit, Deep Cut, Silver Lining) need their unique mechanics implemented.

title: Extend battle.js move effect system for the 26-move NPC pool
description: The current effect model supports three variants (disrupt, self_damage, deferred). The NPC move pool introduces ~12 new effect types: heal, skip_opponent, buff_next (double damage setup), halve_self_next, clear_opponent_buffs, counter (conditional damage), priority (goes first), multi_turn_skip, reveal_next_move, nullify_last_buff, and scope_creep (multi-turn self-damage). Each must be fully specified and implemented in battle.js before student NPC battles can be tested. Effect strings should be defined consistently with the existing convention.
close_reason: All 26 npcMove effects implemented in _applyOpponentTurn; 3 complex effects stubbed with tracking issues; DebugSelectorScene extended with student NPC column

title: Extend data.js with npcMoves, studentNPCs, ambientNPCs, and items arrays
description: data.js needs four new exported arrays before any downstream feature work can begin. npcMoves: the 26-move pool from gdd/student_NPCs.md (effect strings TBD pending move effects issue). studentNPCs: 15 battle characters (Halvorsen through Suzanna) with HP, moveset subsets, dialogue keys, and location data. ambientNPCs: 7 non-battle characters (Casey, Antaeus, Clara, Beckett, Emeryn, Kyle, Ruby) with dialogue keys, item reward ids, and region/tile positions. items: all consumables and persistent items from gdd/items.md — HP restore values are TBD and should be marked as such.
close_reason: Closed

title: Design and implement courtyard code-gate mechanic
description: The courtyard (Parsemore's secret location) is accessible via a code-gated door in the graduate lounge. The mechanic needs designing: what is the code, how does the player obtain it, and how is it checked in map.js. Implement as part of OverworldScene.

title: Battle hit animations and hit sound effects
description: When a professor is hit: their PNG sprite should flash (rapid alpha or tint cycle, ~3 flashes over ~400ms). When the player is hit: the whole canvas should jitter/shake (small rapid x offset, ~300ms). Both animations should trigger at the start of the relevant HP bar drain, playing concurrently with it. Sound effects: a hit SFX should play when damage lands, scaled to one of 2-3 intensity tiers based on damage as a percentage of the target's max HP (e.g. light hit <20%, heavy hit >=20%). A separate short SFX should play each time the player presses the dialogue-advance button. All SFX assets still need to be sourced and added to assets/sounds/ (or assets/sfx/); file paths and track IDs will need to be added to data.js audioTracks.
close_reason: Closed

title: Battle text: advance dialogue via button press instead of auto-timer
description: Currently battle log lines auto-advance after LINE_DISPLAY_MS. This should change so the player manually advances each line with a key press (e.g. Space/Enter), giving them as much time as they need to read before acting. HP bar drain animations and the post-animation pause should remain on their current timers — only the text-hold step changes. The move menu should not reappear until the player has pressed through all lines in the sequence. A prompt indicator (e.g. '▼' or '[SPACE]') should appear on the battle log panel to signal that input is waiting.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix schwaartz sprite path mismatch in data.js
description: data.js references assets/sprites/battle/schwaartz.png but the actual files are schwaartz_l1.png, schwaartz_l2.png, schwaartz_l3.png. BattleScene will show a Phaser missing-texture placeholder for Schwaartz until the path is corrected. Either rename the file to schwaartz.png or update data.js to point to the correct filename.
close_reason: Fixed as part of multi-level sprite implementation — sprite field now points to schwaartz_l1.png and sprites[] array covers all three levels

title: Move player battle sprite to assets/sprites/battle/
description: assets/sprites/battle_alt/player.png exists but tdd/visuals.md specifies the path as assets/sprites/battle/player.png. Move the file so BattleScene.js can load it from the correct path.
close_reason: assets/sprites/battle/ already contains player.png and schwaartz_l1/l2/l3.png at 192×192 px; TDD updated to reflect 192×192 as the canonical battle sprite size

title: Create battle sprites for Prof. Bayesio and Prof. Vec Tor
description: data.js references assets/sprites/battle/bayesio.png and assets/sprites/battle/vec_tor.png but neither file exists yet. The other four professor battle sprites are present. Visual design notes for both professors are in gdd/professors.md. Sprites should match the pixel art style of existing battle sprites.
close_reason: All professors upgraded to sprites[] in data.js; user added battle PNGs for all six professors

title: Source victory and defeat audio jingles
description: data.js defines tracks for 'victory' and 'defeat' (both loop: false, short jingles) but the files assets/music/victory.mp3 and assets/music/defeat.mp3 do not yet exist. These are required before the audio module is fully testable. Jingles should match the retro chiptune style and affectionate tone of the project: victory satisfying (3-5s), defeat gently comic (not punishing).

title: Write coding briefs for remaining modules
description: engine.md brief written this session as the pattern. Need briefs for the five remaining modules: map, battle, dialogue, audio, game. Each brief extracts the relevant data.js values inline and compresses the TDD function specs to a table — replacing the need to load both the TDD and data.js when implementing a module.

title: Finalise game_design.md — resolve all placeholders
description: game_design.md has placeholder items remaining: professor names (tracked in test_project-3gn), visual design notes (test_project-eaf), player move list (test_project-u0w), and post-battle loss dialogue (test_project-gpz). This issue tracks the GDD as a complete deliverable — it can only be closed when all four placeholder issues are resolved.
close_reason: Closed

title: Author TDD files for all seven modules
description: Write one TDD file per module in tdd/. Modules: game.js, engine.js, data.js, map.js, battle.js, dialogue.js, audio.js. Boundaries agreed in session 4.
close_reason: All seven TDD files authored: game, engine, data, map, battle, dialogue, audio.

title: Write DESIGN.md — methodology overview for portfolio
description: A DESIGN.md at the repo root explaining the AI-assisted development methodology used in this project. Should cover: what each artefact is for (GDD, TDD, session logs, Beads issues), why the architecture is flat and explicit, what role AI played at each stage, and how a reviewer can trace decisions from concept to code. This is a portfolio deliverable, not a README.

title: Define player move list
description: The player needs a fixed set of moves (suggest 4) available throughout the game. Each move needs a name, damage value, and one-line description for the battle UI. Moves should be thematically grounded in linguistics/academia and feel distinct from each other.
close_reason: Closed

title: Name all six professors
description: game_design.md has six professors with [placeholder] names. Each name should reflect their subfield and fit the affectionate, whimsical tone. Professors: 1-Phonetics, 2-Syntax, 3-Semantics, 4-DSA, 5-NLP/Deep Learning, 6-Computational Semantics.
close_reason: Closed

title: Define testing strategy
description: We need a plan for how to test the game. This issue covers framework selection, test process design, and any skills or agents needed to support testing. The game is vanilla JS with a flat, explicit architecture — testing tooling must fit that constraint (no heavy frameworks that introduce complexity for a non-specialist reader). Decisions: what test runner/framework to use, what to test (unit vs integration vs manual), how tests are run (CI, local hook, manual), and whether a dedicated testing skill or agent is warranted.
close_reason: Testing strategy defined and implemented this session: browser-based runner (tests/runner.js), no dependencies, served via python3 -m http.server. data.test.js written with 35 passing tests.

title: Define success criteria
notes: Define project-specific validation conditions as success criteria become clear.
close_reason: VC-01 through VC-11 confirmed in project_charter.md; added VC-10 (castle gate) and VC-11 (portfolio documentation).

title: Fully implement reveal_next (dataset_leak move)
description: The dataset_leak move uses effect: 'reveal_next', described as revealing the opponent's next move. Currently stubbed with flavor text. Requires design decision: since the NPC fires second and player has already committed, reveal_next logically applies to the following turn — show the player which move the NPC will use next turn and enforce it.

title: Fully implement priority (slack_message move)
description: The slack_message move uses effect: 'priority', meaning the user always acts first the following turn. Currently stubbed. Requires a turn-order resolution step in resolveTurn() — the current architecture always applies player move before NPC move, so priority needs a flag that conditionally reorders execution.

title: Fully implement swap_effect (methodology_pivot move)
description: The methodology_pivot move uses effect: 'swap_effect', which re-applies the last-used move's effect to the next move. Currently stubbed in battle.js with flavor text. Requires tracking last-used effect across turns in battleState, then applying it to the move object or damage calculation on the following turn.

title: Balance student NPC HP values
description: All 15 studentNPCs currently use hp: 80 as a uniform placeholder. A balancing pass is needed to tune HP values against professor HP (60–200) and intended encounter difficulty. Requires gameplay testing once student NPC battles are implemented.

title: Clarify Antaeus reward item in GDD
description: Antaeus (ambient NPC) gives 'grad lounge food' per the GDD but no specific item id is assigned. The ambientNpcs.js entry currently omits the reward field. Needs GDD clarification before the data entry can be completed.

title: Resolve full-restore encoding for Carl's Large Cheese Steak Sub
description: Carl's Large Cheese Steak Sub is a full HP restore per the GDD, but the item schema uses effect.value: null for both TBD values and full-restore semantics. The engine will need to distinguish these, either by treating null as full-restore for this specific item or by adding a fullRestore boolean to the Item schema.

title: Implement badge and condition-based reward system
description: Badges are awarded by two mechanisms: post-battle grants (Dialecters Badge from Suzanna, Music League Badge from Lionel, Chicken Run Badge from Composita — these overlap with the student NPC battles issue and should be coordinated) and condition-based awards checked at runtime (Meal Lovers Badge: find all food items and speak to Casey; Community Badge: speak to every NPC including Parsemore). Requires: badge state tracking in game state, condition evaluation logic for the two runtime badges, and a display surface (inventory screen or HUD element) so the player can see collected badges.

title: Implement area gating via key items (ID Card, Secret Code)
description: Two areas require key item checks before entry. ID Card: player must have acquired it from Prof. Schwaartz before building interiors are accessible — currently connections have no conditional logic. Secret Code: courtyard entry requires the code obtained from Vec Tor after defeating him. The castle is already gated by defeated-professor count in map.js; the same pattern needs extending to support key-item state flag checks (hasIdCard, hasSecretCode). A clear failure message should display when the player attempts to enter a gated area without the required item.

title: Implement ambient NPC interactions (dialogue + item give)
description: Seven ambient NPCs (Casey, Antaeus, Clara, Beckett, Emeryn, Kyle, Ruby) need a non-battle interaction mode: player walks adjacent and presses interact, dialogue fires via the existing dialogue system, and an item is added to the player's inventory on dialogue end. The dialogue system exists but has no item-give hook. Dialogue sequences for all seven NPCs need to be added to data.js. Some NPCs give items repeatably (Clara → Triscuits, Emeryn → Purim Cookies); others give once only. This distinction needs to be tracked in game state.

title: Update GDD professors.md for Parsemore
description: Parsemore's entry in gdd/professors.md still reflects her reserve status (HP 120, location TBD). Needs updating to: secret boss status, HP 200, courtyard location (code-gated via graduate lounge).

title: Resolve index.html vs game.html before final build
description: VC-01 in the charter says to open index.html directly. index.html is now the dev hub; the game starts from game.html. Before final deliverable, either restore index.html as the game entry point or update VC-01.

title: Source battle SFX files (hit_light, hit_heavy, dialogue_adv)
description: Three SFX files need to be sourced and placed in assets/sfx/ before battle sound effects will play. The code is already wired up (data.js audioTracks entries, _playHitSfx() and sfx_dialogue_adv in BattleScene.js). Files needed: hit_light.mp3 (light hit, <20% max HP damage), hit_heavy.mp3 (heavy hit, >=20% max HP damage), dialogue_adv.mp3 (short click/beep for dialogue advance keypress). All should be royalty-free. Once placed in assets/sfx/, no code changes are required.

title: Add overworld sprite paths to professor objects in data.js
description: tdd/visuals.md specifies a two-field sprites object on each professor (sprites.battle and sprites.overworld), but data.js only has a single sprite field pointing to the battle sprite. The overworld sprite field needs to be added to each professor object once overworld sprites are produced. This is a data schema gap, not a rendering gap — OverworldScene will need sprites.overworld to render professor NPCs on the map.

title: Update TDDs to reflect gaps found during data.js implementation
description: Two gaps surfaced when writing data.js: (1) tdd/data.md does not list professorMoves in its exports table, but battle.js requires it to resolve professor move ids to damage values — the omission needs correcting. (2) tdd/battle.md defines only three move effects (disrupt, self_damage, deferred) but player moves require five additional effect strings (skip, player_recoil, clear_debuff, halve_next, counter) — the battle TDD needs a player move effects section.

title: Write post-battle loss dialogue for all professors
description: game_design.md has [placeholder] loss dialogue for several professors. Each professor needs 1-2 lines of post-battle dialogue for when the player loses. Tone: affectionate, encouraging, consistent with each professor's personality.
close_reason: Closed

title: Define professor visual designs
description: game_design.md has [placeholder] visual design notes for all six professors. Each entry needs sprite description: colours, distinguishing features, retro pixel style cues. Should fit the whimsical, nerdy tone and be distinct enough to tell professors apart at a glance.
close_reason: Closed

title: Define project deliverables
notes: Enumerate discrete outputs the project must produce. For each deliverable added, create a corresponding Beads issue and record its ID in the Issue: field.
close_reason: Four deliverables defined in project_context/deliverables.md with linked Beads issues.

title: BattleScene.js has no unit test coverage (architectural constraint)
description: BattleScene.js cannot be unit tested with the current browser-based test runner — Phaser scenes require a live DOM, WebGL context, and the full Phaser runtime. The new logic added in S11 (_advanceCallback/_showLine advance mechanism, _playHitSfx tier selection, from > to damage guards, _flashProfSprite) is therefore untested at the unit level. Addressing this would require a browser automation setup (e.g. Playwright) which is out of scope for the current proof-of-concept target. For now, BattleScene behavior must be verified manually in the browser. This issue exists to document the gap.
close_reason: Superseded by test_project-1d6 (P1): full BattleScene test suite with Phaser mock strategy agreed and filed.
